error.regex: (setState.*called after dispose|setState\(\) called in.*State.*no longer appears|This widget has been unmounted)

verdict.summary: setState called on a widget that is no longer in the widget tree. Async callback fires after widget is disposed.

dead_ends.action: Wrap setState in try/catch to silence the error
dead_ends.why_fails: Masks the lifecycle bug; the async operation is still running and wasting resources after dispose

dead_ends.action: Add mounted check only at the setState call site
dead_ends.why_fails: Root cause is async operation not being cancelled in dispose(); it keeps running in background

workarounds.action: Use mounted check combined with proper cleanup pattern
workarounds.how: if (mounted) setState(() { ... }); — but also cancel the underlying async work in dispose()

workarounds.action: Convert to StatelessWidget with provider/bloc for state management
workarounds.how: Move async logic to a provider or cubit; widget just rebuilds from stream, no manual setState needed
